name: Chatham Saw Mill
description: One of the oldest and largest sawmills in Asia, located on Chatham Island and connected to Port Blair by a bridge, offering a fascinating glimpse into colonial-era timber industry.
history: Established by the British in 1883, this sawmill processed timber from the dense Andaman forests and played a key role in the colonial economy.
famousFor: Colonial-era industrial heritage and wood processing museum
